default.name: default
default.description: Default triplet extraction prompt with explicit and implicit triplets
default.prompt:
Extract triplets from the following context. Each triplet must be of the form (entity, relationship, value). Rules: - Entities is the subject of a sentence. Do not use pronouns (e.g he, or it) to describe an entity, use the full descriptive name. - Relationships refer to any characteristic of an entity. Even if a relationship is not explicity mentioned, you must included it. Relationships can be things that entity was involved in, or any descriptive characterisitc of that entity. If an entity is described with multiple characteristics in one go, create seperate triplet entries for each of those characteristics. Example: from the text 'Albert Einstein was a German theoretical physicist best known for developing the theory of relativity.' the triplets are (Albert Einstein, nationality, Germany), (Albert Einstein, occupation, theoretical physicist), (Albert Einstein, best known for, developping the theory of relativity) For example, from the text 'In 1796, Napoleon commanded a military campaign against the Austrians and their Italian allies in the War of the First Coalition' You would extract: (Napoleon, commanded a military campaign in 1796 against, the Austrians and their Italian allies in the War of the First Coalition), (Napoleon, commanded a military campaign against the Austrians and their Italian allies in the War of the First Coalition, in 1796), (Napoleon, commanded a military campaign against Austrians and Italians, in the war of the first coalition in 1796). 
 Given these examples, generate explicit and implicit triplets from the following context. 
 Rules: 
 - The relationships should be as precise as possible, while not needing external information to look them up. 
 - Do NOT include mutliple (entity, relation) pairs which have different values, in this case the relationship or entity is not precicse enough, you must add more precision. 
 Here is the context: 

 {context}

 Given these examples, generate explicit and implicit triplets from the following context. 
 Rules:

 - Do NOT include mutliple (entity, relation) pairs which have different values, in this case the relationship or entity is not precicse enough, you must add more precision. 
 Here is the context: 

 {context}

sft.name: sft
sft.description: Used for SFT and GRPO for qwen model for database creation. Simple since the model is finetuned.
sft.prompt:
Please extract knowledge triplets from the context.
Context:

{context}

Triplets:

sft_with_question.name: sft_with_question
sft_with_question.description: Like sft, but also provides the question so the model can focus on relevant triplets.

zero_rl.name: zero_rl
zero_rl.description: Used for RL on base model for database creation.
zero_rl.prompt:
Extract factual triplets from the following context. Each triplet must be of the form (entity, relationship, value).

Rules:
- The entity should be the subject of the fact.
- Do not use pronouns (e.g., he, she, it, they). Always use the full descriptive name of the entity.
- The relationship should describe a specific characteristic, property, role, action, affiliation, location, time-specific fact, or involvement of the entity.
- If one sentence describes multiple characteristics of the same entity, extract them as separate triplets.
- Include both explicit and reasonably inferable implicit triplets, but do not use external knowledge.
- Relationships should be as precise and self-contained as possible.
- Prefer concise, schema-like relationship phrases such as: "is located in", "was founded in year", "has affiliation", "current president is", "ranked as".
- Do NOT produce multiple vague (entity, relationship) pairs with different values. If multiple values appear, make the relationship more specific.
- Output each triplet on a new line in the exact format:
entity<TAB>relationship<TAB>value
- Do not number the triplets.
- Do not output anything other than the triplets.

Examples:
Text: "Albert Einstein was a German theoretical physicist best known for developing the theory of relativity."
Output:
Albert Einstein	nationality	Germany
Albert Einstein	occupation	theoretical physicist
Albert Einstein	best known for	developing the theory of relativity

Text: "In 1796, Napoleon commanded a military campaign against the Austrians and their Italian allies in the War of the First Coalition."
Output:
Napoleon	commanded a military campaign in 1796 against	the Austrians and their Italian allies
Napoleon	was involved in	War of the First Coalition
Napoleon	commanded military campaign during	1796

Now extract triplets from the following context.

Context:
{context}

formatted_zero_rl.name: formatted_zero_rl
formatted_zero_rl.description: Improved zero-RL prompt for database creation. Uses parenthesized (entity, relationship, value) format to avoid tab-sensitivity in batched inference.
formatted_zero_rl.prompt:
Extract factual triplets from the following context.

Output format — one triplet per line, nothing else:
(entity, relationship, value)

Rules:
- Entity is always the subject. Never use pronouns; use full descriptive names.
- Relationship describes a specific property, role, action, affiliation, date, or involvement.
- One sentence with multiple facts → multiple separate triplets.
- Include both explicit and reasonably inferable implicit facts. No external knowledge.
- Relationships must be precise and self-contained.
- If the same (entity, relationship) pair could have multiple values, make the relationship more specific.
- Output ONLY the triplets. No numbering, no explanation, no other text.

Examples:
Text: "Albert Einstein was a German theoretical physicist best known for developing the theory of relativity."
(Albert Einstein, nationality, Germany)
(Albert Einstein, occupation, theoretical physicist)
(Albert Einstein, best known for, developing the theory of relativity)

Text: "In 1796, Napoleon commanded a military campaign against the Austrians and their Italian allies in the War of the First Coalition."
(Napoleon, commanded military campaign in year, 1796)
(Napoleon, commanded military campaign against, Austrians and their Italian allies)
(Napoleon, was involved in, War of the First Coalition)

Context:
{context}

Triplets:/no_think

Context:
